title: EFT: Empathic Exploration
description: Three practical clinician-facing slides for empathic exploration in EFT.

sources.label: Watson and Sharbanee, Emotion Focused Therapy: Case Conceptualization and Treatment

slides.title: Marker: an edge is forming
slides.body: Use exploration when the client is near experience but cannot yet stay with it.
bullets: A hint, image, bodily signal, or unfinished sentence appears.
The client circles the same place without symbolizing it.
Primary emotion is hidden behind explanation or story.

slides.title: Work at the client's live edge
slides.body: The intervention is precise following: close enough to deepen, loose enough for the client's organismic process to lead.
steps: Reflect the most alive phrase, gesture, tone, or bodily cue.
Ask for one concrete moment rather than the whole life pattern.
Conjecture tentatively and give the client easy authority to correct you.
Move between words, body, image, need, and action tendency.
Mark micro-shifts: softening, tears, anger, breath, stillness, clarity.
Let meaning emerge after contact, not before it.

slides.title: Choose the response mode by process
rows: A felt shift or better word.
Defensiveness if you move too fast.
